community.title: Community
community.description: Share what you're working on, ask for help, and learn together.
community.empty: No posts yet. Be the first to share something.
community.error: Could not load the community feed

comments.empty: No comments yet. Start the conversation.

chat.title: Community chat
chat.description: Chat live with the community, or message the founder directly.
chat.signInRequired: Sign in with an active membership to join the chat.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: FTES AOS is built by one person — your question reaches the person who makes it. Pick the fastest route below, or send a message.
contact.responseTime: We usually reply within 24 hours (support hours Mon–Fri, 9:00–18:00).

form.title: Send a message
form.name: Full name
form.successBody: Thanks. We'll get back to you by email as soon as we can.
form.errorTitle: Couldn't send your message. Please try again.

faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.q1: How soon will I get a reply?
faq.a1: Usually within 24 hours, during support hours Mon–Fri 9:00–18:00.
faq.q2: I can't log in or forgot my password?
faq.a2: Use "Forgot password" on the sign-in screen to reset it. If you're still stuck, send the form with your account email.
faq.a3: Send the form with the "Course / account support" reason and your transaction id, and we'll look into it.
faq.q4: Want to partner or hire FTES AOS learners?
faq.a4: Pick the "Partnership / hiring" reason when sending the form, or message the founder directly on LinkedIn.
faq.q5: Questions about the courses, or whether FTES AOS is right for you?
faq.a5: See the FAQ on the home page — it covers the roadmap, who it's for, and how learning works in detail.
faq.landingLink: See course FAQ on the home page

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: AI-Learning FTES — terms and conditions of use.
terms.body: The full terms document is being finalized. By using FTES AOS you agree to use the platform for lawful learning and to respect the ownership of course content. A detailed version will be published on this page.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: AI-Learning FTES is committed to protecting your personal information.
privacy.body: The full privacy policy is being finalized. FTES AOS collects only the data needed to run your learning (account, progress, submissions) and does not sell your data to third parties. A detailed version will be published on this page.

cookieConsent.title: Cookies
cookieConsent.body: We use necessary cookies to sign you in and remember your language, plus analytics cookies (Google Analytics) to improve your experience. You can accept, reject, or customize.
cookieConsent.modalBody: Choose which cookies you allow. Necessary cookies are always on so the system works.
cookieConsent.necessaryHint: Sign-in, security and language. Always on.
cookieConsent.analyticsHint: Google Analytics — anonymous metrics to improve the product.

note.prompt: Summarize the current lesson into a concise study note. Format it in Markdown with: a 2–3 sentence overview, a bullet list of the key points, and a “Remember” section. Base it only on this lesson's content. Answer in the same language as the lesson.

flashcard.empty: No cards yet. Tap “Generate flashcards” to start.
flashcard.prompt: Based on the current lesson, generate 8 Q&A flashcards to test the learner, in the same language as the lesson. Return ONLY a valid JSON array where each item is {"q":"question","a":"short answer"}. Do not add any text outside the JSON.

home.intro: IT courses are everywhere online, but content that is concise and focuses on practical thinking is rare.
home.intro2: FTES AOS was founded to change that.
home.intro3: We focus on building programming thinking - from Fullstack, DevOps, Security to Solution Architect - with a clear and practical roadmap.

diff.title: What makes FTES AOS different?
diff.des1: FTES AOS focuses on the essence of the IT industry. We don't just teach knowledge, but also train standard programming thinking and system design skills - core competencies that help you develop sustainably in the AI era.
diff.des3: Technology changes constantly and knowledge is everywhere. We focus on teaching the fundamentals, keywords, and thinking skills so that after the course, you can research, read docs, and expand on any stack you want.
diff.des5: FTES AOS operates on a lean model, eliminating unnecessary costs to keep tuition affordable. Our goal is to create a high-quality learning environment that anyone serious about the profession can join.
diff.des6: We teach you to build truly valuable projects and provide long-term support throughout your career development.

githubTeamGate.title: Join your course team
githubTeamGate.linkFirst: First, link your GitHub account so we can invite you to the course team.
githubTeamGate.requestDescription: You're enrolled but not yet in your course GitHub team. Request to join, then accept the invite on GitHub to continue.
githubTeamGate.acceptHint: After requesting, open GitHub and accept the team invitation, then press re-check.
githubTeamGate.warningTitle: You're not in the course GitHub team yet
githubTeamGate.warningBody: Until you join, you can't open the course's premium content — it lives in the course GitHub repo.
githubTeamGate.step1: Press "Request to join team" below — we'll send you the invite.
githubTeamGate.step2: Open GitHub and accept the invitation: from the invite email, or at github.com/orgs/FTES AOS-Academy → Invitations.
githubTeamGate.step3: Come back here and press "I've accepted — re-check".

guide.levelFormula: Total XP to reach level L = {factor} × (L − 1)².

guide.streakRepair: Streak Repair: within {window}h of a reset, restore for 10 × days (cap {cap} coins).

guide.dailyGoalRule: Daily goal: complete {target} learning actions → +{xp} XP.

hero.subline: FTES guides you from the home page into a subject workplace, through a course, into practice with AI, and lands on real outcomes: projects, honors, and a career.

founder.name: Nguyễn Anh Khoa
founder.role: Founder & CEO @ FTES Education · CEO TEDO Technology
founder.quote: Students get hooked by my energy, experience and funny stories — and understand every lesson perfectly!!

newStudentOffer.q: What offers does FTES have for new students?
newStudentOffer.a:
New students get an offer based on total course value:
- Combos of 800,000đ–1,199,000đ: 100,000đ off
- Combos of 1,200,000đ–1,599,000đ: 200,000đ off
- Combos from 1,600,000đ: 300,000đ off plus a licensed copy of Cursor (subject to the running program)

liveClass.q: What do I get when I enroll in a Live class on Zoom?
liveClass.a:
In a Live class you get:
- To learn directly with a Mentor
- The Mentor's support throughout the course
- Access to all Basic class lecture videos (worth 300,000đ) to review anytime

group.q: Is there a discount for enrolling as a group?
group.a:
Yes. FTES applies offers by group size:
- Groups of 2–4: 20% off per student
- Groups of 5–10: 25% off per student
- Groups over 10: 35% off per student

returningStudent.q: What offers do returning students get?
returningStudent.a:
Returning students get several perks:
- Refer a new student successfully: 100,000đ per student (referrer +70 reward points, referred student +30 points)
- Enroll in your next course: 5% off
- Pre-order your next course while still studying: 10% off

scholarship.q: Does FTES offer scholarships or student honors?
scholarship.a:
Yes. FTES runs several programs to encourage learning:
- Top Scholar of FTES (highest score across the center): free next term + 2,000,000đ
- Top of Class (highest score in class): free next course
- Diligence (full attendance + all assignments completed): 10% off tuition
- Talent Scholarship: 2,000,000đ + all FTES courses free + a chance to be trained on and join real projects right after term 5

afterCourse.q: After finishing a course, can I join real projects?
afterCourse.a: Yes. Once you finish the courses and meet the professional level required, you join an interview to be selected into FTES's real-project training class.

installment.q: Does FTES support paying tuition in installments?
installment.a:
Yes. You can pay 30%, 40% or 50% upfront, with the rest completed within 1 month:
- Pay 50%: instant access to past lecture videos + all materials
- Pay 40%: instant access to past lecture videos
- Pay 30%: no early access to videos and materials

refund.q: In what cases does FTES refund 100% of tuition?
refund.a: FTES commits to refunding 100% of tuition if you don't understand the lessons, the teaching method doesn't fit, or you feel the course isn't effective — provided you attend all sessions and complete all required homework.

trial.q: Can I try before officially enrolling?
trial.a: Yes. You get 2 free foundational sessions to try before deciding to enroll.

trialTest.q: After the trial, do I get a tuition discount?
trialTest.a:
Yes. After the 2 trial sessions, you take a placement test and get an offer based on the result:
- 6.0–6.9: 10% off
- 7.0–8.9: 15% off
- 9.0–10: 30% off

combineOffers.q: Can I combine multiple offers at once?
combineOffers.a: Whether offers can be stacked depends on the policy in effect at the time. Please contact the FTES advisory team for guidance on the most beneficial program for you.

cta.title: Ready to start your journey?
cta.subline: Pick a course, step into your subject workplace, and let FTES walk you to real outcomes.

hero.headline: Knowledge & Thinking to conquer every <accent>interview</accent>
hero.subline:
Back then, interns could start with small tasks: fixing UI, writing CRUD APIs, and boilerplate code.
In the AI era, those tasks are already solved in seconds.
Companies pay top salaries for practical Thinking & Knowledge — the part AI still cannot replace.
hero.diagramCaption: Spot where it breaks. Design systems that won’t break.
hero.ctaFounder: Meet the founder of FTES AOS

stats.title: What FTES AOS has
stats.intro: Data is updated in real time from the system.

courses.title: Three tracks. One industry-standard mindset.
courses.intro: Each track is built with strong structure and differs by level, but they share one disciplined mindset: we do not mislead learners for money. Every piece of content is carefully crafted.

fullstack.title: Fullstack
fullstack.desc: APIs · auth · caching · realtime · payments, plus frontends that survive real traffic.

systemDesign.title: System Design
systemDesign.desc: Sharding · replication · consensus · idempotency — design for failure.

devops.title: DevOps & Cloud
devops.desc: CI/CD · Docker · Kubernetes · multi-cloud · observability · on-call.

roadmap.title: Three mastery tracks — learn with structure
roadmap.intro: Each track runs foundation → application with real capstone projects. Follow in order, or pick your track.

fullstack.label: Fullstack
fullstack.caption: Build the whole product — backend to frontend

systems.title: Real systems you build with your own hands
systems.intro: Every capstone is a real production system — not a to-do app.

founder.title: The blunt truth about tech
founder.name: Nguyễn Văn Tự Cường
founder.titles: CTO @ Nivo Technology · CTO @ TEDO Technology
founder.quote: Relax boss — I can still fix the bug.
founder.body1: I teach exactly what I ship in production every day — not slides, real systems at CTO scale.
founder.body2: Everything here is built in the open — the platform, the curriculum, the challenges. Read the engineering notes and judge the work for yourself.
founder.thesis: Plenty of places teach coding to make you feel good and earn a certificate fast. FTES AOS is different — I only tell the blunt truths about this industry and what real systems actually demand.
founder.truth1: Big-project experience does not guarantee interview success.
founder.fix1: Many people ship large projects daily, yet still cannot draw a clear distributed architecture in interviews. I teach you to reason about architecture and defend it clearly.
founder.truth2: Memorizing Microservices theory still gets you rejected when you're pushed on fundamentals.
founder.fix2: Strong interviewers do not ask definitions. They drill into data consistency, network failures, and single points of failure. I train you to understand the root so you can hold your ground.
founder.truth3: Using template answers from blogs is the fastest way to get exposed in interviews.
founder.fix3: Textbook architectures often collapse against real business problems. I teach you independent thinking so you can handle any variation.
founder.truth4: The market has no room left for fluff — no one pays Senior salary for keyword name-dropping.
founder.fix4: Companies are tightening standards hard. I train you in real architecture capability so you can pass tough screening rounds with confidence.

recruiter.title: Hire engineers who've proven it
recruiter.intro: Learners who finished the work and opted in to be hired — vetted by what they built, not by a résumé.
recruiter.count: {count}+ engineers open to work right now

engineer.title: For users
engineer.body: You build a portfolio from real, production-grade projects, and I take it straight to hiring teams. They call you for what you can do, not for a polished CV.

enterprise.title: For companies
enterprise.body: Skip inflated CVs. Hire people with real systems-thinking depth.

faq.title: Questions, answered honestly
faq.q1: Is this another bootcamp promising a high salary in 3 months?
faq.a1: No. I don't sell a salary number. I train architectural thinking and prove it with graded challenges — the rest is on you and the market.
faq.q2: Will AI make these skills obsolete?
faq.a2: The opposite. AI writes CRUD already. What it can't do is own trade-offs, design for scale and defend decisions — which is exactly what I train.
faq.q3: I'm a beginner. Is this for me?
faq.a3: If you can already write basic code and want to level up to designing systems, yes. If you've never coded, start with fundamentals first.
faq.q4: How does the AI grading actually work?
faq.a4: Each challenge is scored against production-grade criteria with written feedback. You see exactly why you passed or what to fix.
faq.a5: Opt in as open-to-work and your graded work becomes visible to hiring partners browsing the talent directory. No guarantees — just real signal.

closing.title: No more learning just to know. Time to build real systems.
closing.subtitle: Train to do real work, with enough grit to clear every technical interview in the language you already use.

learnLoop.title: Learn with my method.
learnLoop.intro: In interviews, what matters is not years of experience, but your problem-solving mindset. I systematized the industry's core knowledge into this passion project and built an effective learning method so more people can access it and break through.

grade.title: AI-graded challenge
grade.desc: Write code, scored on correctness & design.

capstone.title: Capstone
capstone.desc: Ship a production-grade system, not a toy.

rank.title: Leaderboard
rank.desc: Compare solutions and climb the ranks.

treasure.title: Learn to solve real problems, not to memorize answers.
treasure.intro: From backend to infrastructure, you train end-to-end problem-solving mindset — the thing that decides whether you survive on large projects.
treasure.interconnect: Idempotency, learned in System Design — reused in Payment, Webhooks (Fullstack).
treasure.beaconTap: Auto-reshuffles every 10s — tap to reshuffle now

graph.cap: Trade consistency vs availability under network partitions — choose per system.
graph.sharding: Split data across shards to scale writes/reads beyond one node.
graph.saga: Long transactions across services via compensating steps, no 2PC.
graph.idempotency: Same result no matter how many times a call retries.
graph.redlock: Distributed lock on Redis + fencing token to stop client races.
graph.kafka: Append-only log as source of truth: replay, decouple producers/consumers.
graph.cdc: Capture DB changes (Debezium) to a stream — no dual writes.
graph.token-bucket: Smooth rate limiting with token buckets + atomic Redis Lua.
graph.websocket: Two-way realtime gateway, fan-out events to thousands of clients.
graph.tracing: Follow one request across services to find bottlenecks (OTel/Jaeger).
graph.nestjs: Modular backend framework with clean DI for large APIs.
graph.typeorm: TypeScript ORM: entities, relations, migrations — close to SQL.
graph.webhooks: Receive third-party events safely: verify signature + idempotent.
graph.payment: Integrate payment gateways: idempotency, reconciliation, refunds.
graph.rag: Semantic search with pgvector, then grounded LLM answers with sources.
graph.oauth: Auth and authorization via OAuth2/JWT with safe refresh tokens.
graph.jobs: Background job queues: retry, backoff, scheduling — off the request path.
graph.rsc: React Server Components: render on the server, ship less JS.
graph.k8s: Container orchestration: self-healing, scaling, declarative rollouts.
graph.ingress: Route HTTP into the cluster: host/path, TLS, load balancing.
graph.argocd: GitOps: desired state in Git, ArgoCD syncs it to the cluster.
graph.rollouts: Canary/blue-green deploys that analyze metrics before full traffic.
graph.prometheus: Collect metrics and alert in real time for running systems.
graph.rbac: Role-based access in K8s: who can do what, least privilege.
graph.terraform: Infrastructure as code, multi-cloud, reproducible plan/apply.
graph.falco: Detect abnormal container runtime behavior in real time.
graph.consistentHashing: Consistent hashing: add/remove nodes with minimal re-keying — the base for elastic sharding.
graph.circuitBreaker: Trip the circuit when a dependency keeps failing — stop cascading failures.
graph.eventSourcing: Store the event stream as source of truth; rebuild state by replay.
graph.readReplica: Read-only replicas absorb read load — at the cost of replication lag.
graph.graphql: Typed API: clients fetch exactly the fields they need from one endpoint.
graph.redisCache: Cache-aside on Redis: TTL, invalidation, stampede protection.
graph.sse: Server-Sent Events: lightweight one-way server push for streams.
graph.zodValidation: Runtime validation + inferred types with Zod at the API edge.
graph.helm: Package K8s manifests into reusable charts with per-env values.
graph.autoscaling: HPA scales pods by CPU/custom metrics — tracks real load.
graph.serviceMesh: A mesh layer handling mTLS, retries, and traffic-splitting between services.
graph.grafana: Visual dashboards + alerts on top of Prometheus metrics.

courseInterview.title: AI interview
courseInterview.subtitle: Take an end-of-course AI interview — MCQ, essay, and oral viva — then receive your score and feedback.
courseInterview.entrySubtitle: End-of-course AI interview with MCQ, essay, and oral viva
courseInterview.overviewBody: Answer multiple-choice, essay, and oral questions. The oral section is an interactive conversation with an AI examiner.
courseInterview.notReadyBody: This course's interview question set is not ready yet. Please check back later.
courseInterview.emptyBody: The instructor hasn't created an AI interview for this course yet. Please check back later.

courseInterview.forbiddenBody: Enroll in the course to unlock the AI interview.

submission.title: Submit your work
submission.subtitle: Submit for automatic grading.
submission.settingsHint: Pick a grader and check remaining credits.
submission.mockFeedback: Submission passed. Good structure — consider adding tests for edge cases.
submission.noResult: You haven't submitted yet. Paste your URL and press Submit to be graded.
submission.error: Failed to load the submission page.
submission.quotaReached: Quota reached. Upgrade to keep grading.

codeGrading.testCasesHint: Enter input and expected output — your code runs against each case (objective, model-independent).
codeGrading.modelHint: Each model grades differently — test-case results are objective, the score/feedback depends on the model.
codeGrading.regradeHint: Switch model and grade again to compare — test-case results are kept while your code is unchanged.
codeGrading.sqlStaticOnly: SQL is graded by static analysis — test cases are not executed.
codeGrading.sqlNoExecution: SQL is graded statically (no test execution).

errors.forbidden: Your plan does not include AI code grading yet. Upgrade your plan to use this feature.
errors.down: The grading service is under maintenance. Please try again in a few minutes — your code is kept as-is.
errors.failed: Grading failed. Try again — your code is kept as-is.
errors.invalid: The submission is invalid — check your code and language.
errors.modelNotAllowed: This model is not on the allowlist — switched back to the default model, please grade again.
errors.generic: Something went wrong while grading. Please try again.
